name: Waimea Bay
tagline: Birthplace of big wave surfing in winter, glass-calm swimming hole in summer — two completely different worlds.
parking: Parking fills by 10am — arrive early or use Haleiwa shuttle
bestTime: Summer for calm swimming and Jump Rock; winter for watching 30+ ft waves (do NOT swim)
talkStory: Waimea means 'reddish water' in Hawaiian, from the red soil washing into the bay. This is the birthplace of big-wave surfing — Greg Noll rode the first massive waves here in 1957, and the Eddie Aikau Big Wave Invitational, held only when swells hit 30+ feet, is surfing's most sacred event. Jump Rock on the left side is a summer rite of passage for local kids. In winter the bay becomes a churning death trap — even wading is deadly. Waimea Valley across the road makes a perfect combo day.
